stocks.name: Zscaler, Inc.
stocks.explanation: Zscaler shares collapsed after the cloud security firm guided Q4 revenue of $875-878 million, below consensus, and slashed full-year free cash flow outlook to 22.8-23.3% from 26.5-27%. Despite beating Q3 estimates on both revenue ($850.5M) and adjusted EPS ($1.08), investors punished the stock on increased capex and sales leadership transitions.

stocks.name: AppLovin Corporation
stocks.explanation: AppLovin surged on a report from Edgewater indicating Meta Platforms is unlikely to bid on non-IDFA iOS traffic in the near term, reducing competitive pressure. The rally was further supported by strong Q1 2026 earnings and optimism around the upcoming public launch of AppLovin's self-serve AXON advertising platform in June.

stocks.name: Qualcomm Incorporated
stocks.explanation: Qualcomm led a broader semiconductor selloff, dropping nearly 8% despite earlier positive news of an agreement to supply AI-focused chips to ByteDance. Investor concern centered on sequential earnings guidance stepping down for Q3 2026, lingering handset market weakness, and the ongoing risk of Apple's vertical integration of modem technology.

stocks.name: Arm Holdings plc
stocks.explanation: Arm Holdings pulled back amid a broad chip sector rotation and profit-taking after the stock nearly doubled year-to-date. Regulatory overhang from FTC antitrust scrutiny into Arm's licensing practices added to selling pressure, though long-term AI and datacenter tailwinds remain intact.

stocks.name: Atlassian Corporation
stocks.explanation: Atlassian continued its post-earnings rally after the company beat Q3 expectations with $1.75 adjusted EPS versus $1.32 expected and $1.79 billion revenue versus $1.69 billion expected. Strong cloud growth and renewed optimism around the software sector's recovery from the 'SaaS-pocalypse' supported the advance.

stocks.name: Sea Limited
stocks.explanation: Sea Limited rallied as Southeast Asian internet stocks continued to attract inflows amid improving e-commerce margins and digital financial services growth. The company has been benefiting from Shopee's profitability momentum and expanding fintech penetration across the region.

stocks.name: Fortinet, Inc.
stocks.explanation: Fortinet retreated in sympathy with Zscaler's guidance-driven collapse, as the cybersecurity sector broadly sold off. A recent DZ Bank downgrade to Hold from Buy on May 18 continued to weigh on sentiment, though Wedbush maintained a constructive long-term outlook on the cyber sector overall.

stocks.name: Marvell Technology, Inc.
stocks.explanation: Marvell pulled back ahead of its Q1 earnings report due after Wednesday's close. Despite HSBC upgrading the stock to Buy with a $300 target on optical interconnect strength, pre-earnings jitters and a broader semiconductor rotation weighed on shares. Analysts expect Q1 revenue of $2.41 billion, up 27% year-over-year.

stocks.name: Strategy (MicroStrategy)
stocks.explanation: Strategy (formerly MicroStrategy) declined as Bitcoin retreated from recent highs, dragging the company's shares lower given its substantial Bitcoin treasury holdings. The stock remains highly correlated with cryptocurrency price movements and broader risk-off sentiment in Wednesday's session.

stocks.name: Intel Corporation
stocks.explanation: Intel fell after Northland downgraded the stock to Market Perform from Outperform, citing full valuation and modeling a decline in overall datacenter spending in calendar year 2027 as hyperscalers become cash-strapped. The downgrade offset earlier turnaround optimism and contributed to the broader chip sector weakness.

stocks.name: CrowdStrike Holdings, Inc.
stocks.explanation: CrowdStrike gave back gains after hitting eight straight intraday records, pulling back in a sector-wide cybersecurity selloff triggered by Zscaler's weak guidance. Despite announcing the successful Glassworm botnet takedown with Google and receiving a Wedbush price target raise to $700, profit-taking prevailed.

stocks.name: MongoDB, Inc.
stocks.explanation: MongoDB declined amid the broader software and cloud infrastructure selloff. The database platform maker was caught in the crosscurrent of renewed SaaS-pocalypse fears following Zscaler's guidance miss, despite the broader software sector's recent 25% rally from April lows.

stocks.name: Cloudflare, Inc.
stocks.explanation: Cloudflare traded lower in sympathy with the broader cloud and cybersecurity sector rotation following Zscaler's guidance-driven collapse. The stock pulled back despite strong fundamentals and Cloudflare's recently launched Flagship developer platform, reflecting risk-off sentiment across growth-oriented infrastructure names.

stocks.name: Pinterest, Inc.
stocks.explanation: Pinterest gained as digital advertising stocks broadly outperformed, benefiting from AppLovin's rally and the report that Meta is pulling back from certain iOS ad inventory. Investors rotated into lower-valuation ad-tech names as the digital ad market shows resilience heading into the summer spending season.

stocks.name: Palo Alto Networks, Inc.
stocks.explanation: Palo Alto Networks pulled back alongside cybersecurity peers following Zscaler's guidance miss, despite Wedbush raising its price target to $300 from $225 on AI-driven security demand. The CEO's recent $10 million insider purchase signaled long-term confidence, but short-term sector headwinds from the Zscaler spillover dominated trading.
